2605.10529.title: PrimeKG-CL: A Continual Graph Learning Benchmark on Evolving Biomedical Knowledge Graphs
2605.10529.paper_summary: Biomedical knowledge graphs underwrite drug repurposing and clinical decision support, yet the upstream ontologies they depend on update on independent cycles that add millions of edges and deprecate hundreds of thousands more between releases. Yet existing continual graph learning has been studied almost exclusively on synthetic random splits of static, generic KGs, a regime that cannot reproduce the asynchronous, structured evolution real biomedical KGs undergo. To this end, we introduce PrimeKG-CL, a CGL benchmark built from nine authoritative biomedical databases (129K+ nodes, 8.1M+ edges, 10 node types, 30 relation types) with two genuine temporal snapshots (June 2021, July 2023; 5.83M edges added, 889K removed, 7.21M persistent), 10 entity-type-grouped tasks, multimodal node features, and a per-task persistent/added/removed test stratification. On three tasks (biomedical relationship prediction, entity classification, KGQA), we evaluate six CL strategies across four KGE decoders, plus LKGE, an LLM-RAG agent, and CMKL. We find that decoder choice and continual learning strategy interact strongly: no single strategy performs best across all decoders, and mismatched combinations can significantly degrade performance. Moreover, only DistMult exhibits a clear separation between persistent and deprecated knowledge, indicating that standard metrics conflate retention of still-valid facts with failure to forget outdated ones; this effect is absent under RotatE. In addition, multimodal features improve entity-level tasks by up to 60%, and a recent CKGE framework (IncDE) failed to scale to our 5.67M-triple base task across five attempts up to 350GB RAM. Data, pipeline, baselines, and the stratified split are released openly. Dataset:huggingface.co/datasets/yradwan147/PrimeKGCL|Code:github.com/yradwan147/primekg-cl-neurips2026
2605.10529.paper_summary_zh: 生物醫學知識圖譜支持藥物再利用和臨床決策支持，然而它們所依賴的上游本體在獨立的週期中更新，這些更新在版本之間增加了數百萬條邊，並淘汰了數十萬條邊。現有的持續圖學習幾乎專門研究靜態、通用知識圖譜的合成隨機拆分，這種模式無法重現真實生物醫學知識圖譜所經歷的非同步、結構化演變。為此，我們介紹了PrimeKG-CL，這是一個基於九個權威生物醫學數據庫構建的CGL基準（129K+ 節點，8.1M+ 邊，10 種節點類型，30 種關係類型），擁有兩個真實的時間快照（2021年6月，2023年7月；增加了5.83M條邊，移除889K條，持久性7.21M），10個實體類型分組任務，多模態節點特徵，以及每個任務的持久/新增/移除測試分層。在三個任務（生物醫學關係預測、實體分類、KGQA）中，我們評估了六種CL策略在四個KGE解碼器上的表現，以及LKGE、一個LLM-RAG代理和CMKL。我們發現解碼器選擇和持續學習策略之間有強烈的互動：沒有單一策略在所有解碼器中表現最佳，且不匹配的組合可能顯著降低性能。此外，只有DistMult顯示出持久知識和淘汰知識之間的明顯區分，這表明標準指標將仍然有效的事實的保留與忘記過時事實的失敗混淆；這一效應在RotatE下不存在。此外，多模態特徵使實體級任務的表現提高了最多60%，而最近的CKGE框架（IncDE）在五次嘗試中未能擴展到我們的5.67M三元組基準任務，最高達到350GB RAM。數據、管道、基準和分層拆分已公開釋出。數據集:huggingface.co/datasets/yradwan147/PrimeKGCL|代碼:github.com/yradwan147/primekg-cl-neurips2026
2605.10529.author: Yousef A. Radwan et.al.
2605.10529.authors: Yousef A. Radwan, Yao Li, Qing Qing, Ziqi Xu, Xingtong Yu, Jiaxing Huang, Renqiang Luo, Xikun Zhang
